LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CSK AUTO, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. STIPULATION AND ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT WHEREIN PARTIES STIPULATED THAT PLAINTIFF TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT IN 20 DAYS; HEARING DATE ON CSK'S DEMURRER CONTINUED TO 12/10/07; AND AFTER PLAINTIFFS FILE AND SERVE AMENDED PLEADING, CSK WILL TAKE ITS DEMURRER OFF CALENDAR. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY AJDUDICATION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE THAT DECEDENT WAS NOT EXPOSED TO DEFENDANT'S ASBESTOS BY BEING EXPOSED TO HAMILTON JOINT COMPOUND. SOPHISTICATED USER DOCTRINE NOT APPLICABLE, HAMILTON IS THE INTERMEDIARY. DEFENDANT PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE BULK SUPPLIES DEFENSE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Oct-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT UNION CARBIDE CORPORATION MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY AJDUDICATION. Appearances: Oren Noah, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLC, (415) 898-1555 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Jann M. Noddin, Esq. of Brydon Hugo & Parker, (415) 808-0300 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift burden of proof. Defendant fails to demonstrate that decedent was not exposed to defendant's asbestos by being exposed to Hamilton joint compound. Sophisticated user doctrine not applicable, Hamilton is the intermediary. Defendant presents no evidence to support the bulk supplies defense. Motion moot as to false representation on punitive damages claims. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2007 Continued To Dec-10-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CSK AUTO, INC.'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT FOR SURVIVAL, WRONGFUL DEATH -ASBESTOS.
APPEARANCES: Christina French, Esq. of Brayton Purcell, LLP 898-1555 for pltfs & opposing parties; Paul S. Lecky, Esq. of Becherer Kannett & Schweitzer 510/658-3600 for deft & moving party.
RULING: The Court vacated its tentative ruling. Stipulation and order signed in open Court wherein parties stipulated that plaintiff to file an amended complaint in 20 days; hearing date on CSK's demurrer continued to 12/10/07; and after plaintiffs file and serve amended pleading, CSK will take its demurrer off calendar. (302/PJM) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
